name: The First National Bank of Johnson
description_short: Community-focused bank serving agricultural and personal banking needs across Johnson and Auburn with mobile banking, ag loans, and home financing options.
description_long: First National Bank of Johnson is a community bank operating branches in Johnson and Auburn that positions itself as a locally-rooted financial institution deeply integrated into its service communities. The bank emphasizes its understanding of local agricultural economies and personal banking needs, using the tagline 'You know farming, and we know banking' to appeal to its core agricultural customer base. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including agricultural loans designed for farming operations, home loans for residential purchases, personal loans for various life needs, and business loans for entrepreneurship and growth. They provide digital banking convenience through mobile banking capabilities including mobile check deposits and bill pay features, as well as traditional services like check reordering and financial calculators. The institution maintains physical branch locations and staffed customer service during business hours. First National Bank of Johnson distinguishes itself through explicit community commitment, stating 'We support our communities because we are part of the community.' Their marketing materials specifically target agricultural customers with specialized loan products, suggesting deeper expertise in farm financing than typical retail banks. The bank is FDIC-insured and an Equal Housing Lender, meeting federal regulatory standards. The bank's website provides limited detailed information about specific loan terms, rates, or fee structures. While they advertise 'Latest Rates,' actual rates are not displayed on the public website, requiring customers to contact the bank directly. The institution appears to be a regional community bank without the digital-first approach or extensive online account opening capabilities of larger national banks or neobanks.
services: Agricultural loans for farming operations and rural enterprises
Personal loans for consolidation and life expenses
Business loans for startups and business growth
Online banking portal with login access
Mobile banking app with check deposit functionality
Remote bill pay and payment scheduling
In-person branch banking at Johnson and Auburn locations
pros: FDIC-insured member bank providing deposit protection up to $250,000
Specialized agricultural loan products tailored to farming operations and rural businesses
Mobile banking app with mobile check deposit and remote bill pay capabilities
Multiple physical branch locations in Johnson and Auburn for in-person service
Community-focused institution with direct ties to local economic development
Equal Housing Lender status ensuring fair lending compliance
Diverse loan portfolio including ag, personal, business, and home loans
cons: Interest rates and loan terms not transparently displayed online—requires direct contact
Limited information about fees, minimum balances, or account requirements on website
No indication of online account opening or digital-only banking options
Geographically limited to Johnson and Auburn branches, not a national bank
Website lacks details on insurance products beyond mention of an 'Insurance' menu item
best_for: Agricultural business owners and farmers seeking specialized ag lending
Homebuyers in Johnson or Auburn looking for local mortgage services
Small business owners wanting relationship-based lending from a community bank
Residents of Johnson and Auburn seeking convenient branch banking with personalized service
diagnosis: First National Bank of Johnson is best for agricultural customers, homebuyers, and small business owners in central Illinois seeking community-based banking with specialized farm lending expertise. The main caveat is that specific loan terms, rates, and fees are not publicly available online, requiring direct contact with the bank for pricing information before commitment.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
fdic_official_name: The First National Bank of Johnson
